channel.title: Posts on Brian Zbriger
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Brian Zbriger

item.title: FlatMonitor: An Experiment in Spec-Driven Development
item.description: I recently released a passion project, FlatMonitor, that was meant to serve multiple goals. While there are a whole lot of open source uptime monitoring tools out there already, I didn’t find one that was a great fit for my use case, and knew I would learn a lot from building my own. At the same time I thought this would be a great opportunity to explore the concept of spec driven development (SDD) that I’ve been hearing so much about recently.

item.title: A Simpler Markdown Resume (with Pandoc + XeLaTeX)
item.description:
Mantaining a resume can be a pain. I’ve tried a lot of setups over the years. A basic word processor is good enough in many respects, but I don’t like the lack of full version control or the fussiness of the formatting. I’ve also tried LaTex, but it’s a lot to learn and I would much rather work in clean Markdown.

If you’re set on Markdown, then the question is how to generate a presentable PDF. Pandoc is a great tool for this but getting a work flow set up and managing the formatting can get rather complicated. I’ve been searching for a way to get a good result with as few moving parts as possible and here’s what I’ve settled on for now.
